name: Asoiaf.Api

aliases.name: BookQuery
aliases.comment: Represents the fields we can query books by
aliases.type: { name : Maybe String, fromReleaseDate : Maybe Date, toReleaseDate : Maybe Date }

aliases.name: CharacterQuery
aliases.comment: Represents the fields we can query characters by
aliases.type: { name : Maybe String, gender : Maybe String, culture : Maybe String, born : Maybe Int, died : Maybe Int, isAlive : Maybe Bool }

aliases.name: HouseQuery
aliases.comment: Represents the fields we can query houses by
aliases.type: { name : Maybe String, region : Maybe String, words : Maybe String, hasWords : Maybe Bool, hasTitles : Maybe Bool, hasSeats : Maybe Bool, hasDiedOut : Maybe Bool, hasAncestralWeapons : Maybe Bool }

aliases.name: Pagination
aliases.comment:
All requests are paginated. Use this to specify which page to request and
what page size to use.

values.name: defaultBookQuery
values.comment: Default book query. No fields specified.

values.name: defaultCharacterQuery
values.comment: Default character query. No fields specified.

values.name: defaultHouseQuery
values.comment: Default house query. No fields specified.

values.name: queryBooks
values.comment:
Query books

    pagination =
      { page = 1
      , pageSize = 25
      }
    query =
      { defaultBookQuery
      | name = Just "A Game of Thrones"
      }
    queryBooks pagination query == [ { name = "A Game of Thrones", ... } ]

values.name: queryCharacters
values.comment:
Query characters

    pagination =
      { page = 1
      , pageSize = 25
      }
    query =
      { defaultCharacterQuery
      | name = Just "Jon Snow"
      }
    queryCharacters pagination query == [ { name = "Jon Snow", ... } ]

values.name: queryHouses
values.comment:
Query houses

    pagination =
      { page = 1
      , pageSize = 25
      }
    query =
      { defaultBookQuery
      | words = Just "Winter is Coming"
      }
    queryHouses pagination query == [ { name = "House Stark of Winterfell", ... } ]

name: Asoiaf.Models

aliases.name: Book
aliases.type: { url : String, name : String, isbn : String, authors : List String, numberOfPages : Int, publisher : String, country : String, mediaType : String, released : String, characters : List String, povCharacters : List String }

aliases.name: Character
aliases.type: { url : String, name : String, gender : String, culture : String, born : String, died : String, titles : List String, aliases : List String, father : String, mother : String, spouse : String, allegiances : List String, books : List String, povBooks : List String, tvSeries : List String, playedBy : List String }

aliases.name: House
aliases.type: { url : String, name : String, region : String, coatOfArms : String, words : String, titles : List String, seats : List String, currentLord : String, heir : String, overlord : String, founded : String, founder : String, diedOut : String, ancestralWeapons : List String, cadetBranches : List String, swornMembers : List String }

values.name: getId
values.comment:
Gets the id from the end of a URL as used in the ASOIAF API.

    getId "http://www.anapioficeandfire.com/api/houses/362" == 362

Useful for for example getting ids of the books a character is appearing in

    getBookIds : Character -> List Int
    getBookIds character =
      List.map getId character.books
